_note: Round-11 follow-up #2: a `dispatch(EV)` forward reference in source order — Inner has `function fire() { dispatch(EV) }` BEFORE `const EV = 'save'`. Pre-fix native ran a separate pre-pass (`scan_statement_for_literal_vars`) that populated literal_vars globally before the dispatched-name scan, so the forward-referenced call wrongly resolved EV='save' and contributed 'save' to the events surface. Upstream's TS walker visits the function body inside its declaration position (deferred to runtime semantically, but visited in source order at parse time), so when `dispatch(EV)` is processed, EV isn't yet in `stringVars`. Native now uses a single source-order walker (`scan_statement_in_source_order`) that grows literal_vars only as it encounters `const NAME = 'literal'` bindings — forward references resolve to nothing. Verify via `--emit-ts`: post-fix the events surface is `{ [evt: string]: CustomEvent<any> }` with no specific 'save' key; pre-fix it would have included `'save': CustomEvent<any>` in synthesized_untyped_events. (Under non-strict mode the consumer-side typecheck doesn't fire either way because the lax index signature accepts any name; the divergence is at the analyze layer and visible in the emit shape.)
